figures.caption: Fig. 3. Results for characterization and synchronization showing: (a) sinusoidal system response characterization, and histograms of estimated residual lag distributions for (b) Operator Action vs State, and (c) State vs Tip Position.
Table 1. Comparison of colonoscopy datasets and resources by available modalities

Fig. 4. Example episode with synchronized multimodal recordings from the robotic colonoscopy dataset. From top to bottom: colonoscope video snapshots sampled from the same time axis as data streams; operator control actions; distal tip pose; robot state (normalized to [−1, 1] per axis for ease of visualization).
Fig. 1. Overview of the proposed OpenRC framework, including hardware interfaces, actuation, data acquisition, and sensing via EM tracking.

Fig. 5. Episode-level characteristics of the OpenRC Dataset showing distributions of (a) episode duration, (b) trajectory length, and (c) recorded task. Detailed task descriptions will be provided in the dataset repository.
Fig. 2. OpenRC components: (a) bending module, (b) feeding module, (c) ROS 2 graph showing data streams, and (d) experimental setup for data collection.
